title: agrostis peninsularis
intro: Agrostis peninsularis, also known as Island Bentgrass, is a species of grass native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it useful in treating wounds and skin irritations. The health benefits of Agrostis peninsularis include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. Herbal preparations of Agrostis peninsularis can be made by infusing the leaves and stems in hot water to create a tea, or by using the plant in topical applications such as salves and ointments.
